schema_notes: Word resume rewrite plus JD coverage matrix and non-invention checks

jd_requirements.requirement: 6+ years production backend experience with Go or Python
PostgreSQL schema design, tuning, or online migration
Kafka or equivalent event stream system
service reliability instincts such as on-call, SLOs, or incident postmortems

factual_errors.expected_fix: End-date must be on or after start-date. The original resume in earlier shipping had '(2019–2022)'; an acceptable correction is restoring '2019–2022' or any chronologically valid range whose end >= start.
factual_errors.verification_rule: Search rewritten resume_v2.docx text for regex '2019-?0?6.*2018-?12' (case-insensitive) — MUST NOT match. Additionally, parse the Bluebird heading's date range and assert end_year_month >= start_year_month.

original_text_anchors: served 8,000 customers across the billing platform
factual_errors.defect: Two bullets in the same role/period claim different customer counts (12k vs 8,000); cannot both be true.
factual_errors.expected_fix: Only one customer-count figure may survive in Meridian Cloud bullets. Acceptable: keep 12k / 12,000 OR keep 8k / 8,000, but not both. Removing both is also acceptable if the rewrite chooses to drop the metric.
factual_errors.verification_rule: Within the Meridian Cloud bullets of resume_v2.docx, count occurrences of regex '\b(12[,\s]?000|12k)\b' and '\b(8[,\s]?000|8k)\b' (case-insensitive). At MOST one of the two patterns may have a non-zero count.

factual_errors.defect: Resume claims 4 years of Kubernetes experience, but Kubernetes appears only in the Meridian Cloud stack (2022–2023), giving at most ~1–2 years. The 4-year claim is mathematically impossible.
factual_errors.expected_fix: Drop the '4 years' claim, restate as '1–2 years' / 'since 2022' / leave Kubernetes mentioned only in skills/stack lines without a year count.
factual_errors.verification_rule: Search rewritten resume_v2.docx text for regex '\b(3|4|5|6|7|8|9|\d{2,})\+?\s*(years?|yrs?)\s+(of\s+)?(hands-on\s+)?Kubernetes' (case-insensitive) — MUST NOT match.
